name: Bay Window
description: A small bay window that protrudes 1-2 blocks outward from a flat wall section. Builds a 3-wide, 2-3 tall box extruded along the chosen `protrude_axis` ('+z' by default). The front face and the two side faces are clad in @glass_pane, the top is closed with a @slab cap, and the bay floor is laid in @primary as a sill. A flower pot and a hanging lantern are placed inside so the alcove reads as a lived-in window seat.

required_furniture.rule: Full @glass_pane curtain on the 3 exposed faces (front + 2 sides) of the protruding box.
@slab course one row above the bay, sealing the top of the protrusion.
@primary floor across the bay footprint — doubles as the window sill.
minecraft:flower_pot on the bay floor, centred toward the front to read from outside.
minecraft:lantern[hanging=true] suspended from the underside of the slab cap (Pools of Light).

placement_rules: Bay protrudes from wall along outward axis (kwarg `protrude_axis`: '+x', '-x', '+z', '-z'; default '+z').
Glass on the 3 exposed faces — front face plus the two side faces; the wall-side face stays open so the bay connects to the room.
Plant + light inside: flower pot on the sill, hanging lantern beneath the slab cap.
Bay is 3 cells wide on the wall plane and 1-2 cells deep along the protrude axis; height tracks the wall section, capped at 3 rows of glass.
Defensive on AABBs from 3x3x1 up to 5x4x2 — out-of-range inputs are clamped, never raised.
lighting_ventilation: The hanging lantern provides interior light (light level 15) and the three glass faces flood the alcove with daylight. Bay windows are a natural 'pool of light' inside an otherwise dim room.

medieval.notes: Oak sill with plain glass — fits cottages, inns, half-timbered houses.

modern.notes: Clean white panes with a quartz sill for a minimalist bay.

fantasy.notes: Stained purple glass and dark oak — fits towers and wizard studies.
